2604.20254.title: Mol-Debate: Multi-Agent Debate Improves Structural Reasoning in Molecular Design
2604.20254.paper_summary: Text-guided molecular design is a key capability for AI-driven drug discovery, yet it remains challenging to map sequential natural-language instructions with non-linear molecular structures under strict chemical constraints. Most existing approaches, including RAG, CoT prompting, and fine-tuning or RL, emphasize a small set of ad-hoc reasoning perspectives implemented in a largely one-shot generation pipeline. In contrast, real-world drug discovery relies on dynamic, multi-perspective critique and iterative refinement to reconcile semantic intent with structural feasibility. Motivated by this, we propose Mol-Debate, a generation paradigm that enables such dynamic reasoning through an iterative generate-debate-refine loop. We further characterize key challenges in this paradigm and address them through perspective-oriented orchestration, including developer-debater conflict, global-local structural reasoning, and static-dynamic integration. Experiments demonstrate that Mol-Debate achieves state-of-the-art performance against strong general and chemical baselines, reaching 59.82% exact match on ChEBI-20 and 50.52% weighted success rate on S$^2$-Bench. Our code is available at https://github.com/wyuzh/Mol-Debate.
2604.20254.paper_summary_zh: 文本引導的分子設計是人工智慧驅動的藥物發現中的一項關鍵能力，然而在嚴格的化學約束下，將連續的自然語言指令映射到非線性的分子結構仍然具有挑戰性。大多數現有的方法，包括RAG、CoT提示以及微調或強化學習，強調在一個主要是一擊即中的生成管道中實施一小組臨時推理視角。相對而言，現實世界的藥物發現依賴於動態的多角度批評和迭代的精煉，以調和語義意圖與結構可行性。受到這一點的啟發，我們提出了Mol-Debate，一種通過迭代生成-辯論-精煉循環來實現此類動態推理的生成範式。我們進一步描述了這一範式中的關鍵挑戰，並通過面向視角的協調來解決這些挑戰，包括開發者-辯論者衝突、全球-局部結構推理以及靜態-動態整合。實驗表明，Mol-Debate在強大的通用和化學基準上達到了最先進的性能，在ChEBI-20上達到59.82%的精確匹配，在S$^2$-Bench上達到50.52%的加權成功率。我們的代碼可在https://github.com/wyuzh/Mol-Debate獲得。
2604.20254.author: Wengyu Zhang et.al.
2604.20254.authors: Wengyu Zhang, Xiao-Yong Wei, Qing Li
